preamble: The diphthongization of [ɛ] to [ai] is a common process in vowel shifts, often occurring as part of a chain shift or through the development of an off-glide.

examples.note: During the Great Vowel Shift, some open-mid front vowels eventually raised and diphthongized towards [ai] through various intermediate stages.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
